text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there sprang up a bright and cheerful garden. Petals like tiny sunflowers danced in the breeze, and the air was humming with the gentle tune of hummingbirds flitting from blossom to blossom.

In the center of this garden stood an old tree, its limbs bowed by time, yet its crown still shone with rich leaves. A young sapling beside it had eyes like glittering gemstones. It whispered secrets to birds that sang round it.

One particular bird, a songbird of extraordinary beauty, would listen closely. Its song was a melody made of summer rain and gentle laughter. Every bird seemed enchanted by the songster.

One day, the songbird swooped down into the earth, tracing a path through the roots until it reached the young sapling. "Well met," it chirped brightly.

"Why hello!" the sapling replied eagerly. "I've heard so many stories about you!"

"Indeed," the songbird replied thoughtfully. "
